goal: PARAMETRIC actuator-tuning canonical: sweep the joint-drive gains (kp, kd, joint_damping) on a Franka articulation to match a reference joint trajectory. UNLIKE CP-NEW-actuator-net-training (A62 — records an excitation in sim and trains a new LSTM motor model from scratch) and UNLIKE CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration (A110 — loads a real-hardware CSV, splits 80/20 train/test, fits an LSTM with held-out validation), this canonical produces NO neural network. The output is a SCALAR (kp, kd, damping) tuple per joint plus a per-joint sweep table — the same drive gains that already exist on the UsdPhysics.DriveAPI, just retuned to minimize per-joint tracking error against a reference trajectory. UNLIKE the pre-existing CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (which uses quick_calibrate to fit armature, Coulomb friction, and link masses — physical actuator constants), this canonical tunes the CONTROL-LOOP GAINS (kp/kd/damping), which are the driver-side coefficients of the same actuator. robot_wizard loads the Franka. monitor_joint_effort captures a 5s effort baseline so the operator can verify the drives are powered before the sweep. get_drive_gains reads the pre-sweep gains as the rollback baseline. create_calibration_experiment runs THREE independent one-parameter sweeps — kp_axis, kd_axis, joint_damping_axis — each over 5 grid points; for each grid point the handler temporarily applies the candidate value via set_drive_gains, replays the reference trajectory, and records per-joint RMSE. validate_calibration computes the post-sweep gap on a held-out reference segment. set_drive_gains finally applies the best (kp, kd, damping) triple per joint. quick_calibrate is NOT used here — it fits physical constants, not drive gains, and would confound the gain-tuning signal. get_joint_positions + get_joint_torques are sampled at the start of each sweep cell for the trajectory replay; monitor_joint_effort runs again at the end as a regression check that the new gains have not saturated any joint. SUCCESS: validate_calibration mean_rmse <= 0.02 rad on the held-out segment AND each joint's selected (kp, kd, damping) yields RMSE below the pre-sweep baseline. Exercises the PARAMETRIC drive-gain calibration pipeline (sweep / apply / validate). Realizes sim-to-real / control-engineer persona.
thoughts: Differentiation matrix — this is the parametric-gain canonical, NOT a neural one. A62 = LSTM-from-scratch with synthetic excitation. A110 = LSTM-fit-from-real-log with 80/20 split + held-out validation. CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator = parametric Bayesian fit of armature/friction/masses (physical constants of the actuator). A113 (this file) = parametric grid sweep over the CONTROL-LOOP gains kp/kd/joint_damping that already live on UsdPhysics.DriveAPI. The output is three scalars per joint, not a neural-network checkpoint.

Pattern is rl-train / physics calibration BUT scoped to drive gains only. simulate_args is null — success is per-joint RMSE under the held-out reference segment, not cube delivery. verify_args is null — no scene build to verify beyond the Franka instantiation. intent.pattern_hint='other' because gain tuning produces a sensitivity table and a best-tuple, not a delivery or training-loop success shape; structural_tags discriminate at retrieval time (isaac:physics.actuator_param_calibration, isaac:calibration.parametric_gains, isaac:sim2real.gain_tuning).

Reference trajectory: a mock CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_reference_trajectory.csv (locally-runnable form). Schema: timestamp_s, joint_0_target..joint_6_target, joint_0_actual..joint_6_actual at 200 Hz, 10s of a planned reach-to-pose. The 'target' columns are what the drive saw; the 'actual' columns are the resulting positions. Per-joint RMSE = sqrt(mean((actual - target)^2)) — this is what the gain sweep minimizes.

Three independent one-axis sweeps, not a 3D grid: a full crossed grid (5×5×5 = 125 cells × 10s replay = 21 min per joint × 7 joints = 2.5 h) is impractical for an interactive canonical. The marginal sensitivity per axis is what we want — kp dominates steady-state tracking, kd dominates overshoot, joint_damping dominates oscillation. Sweeping crossed misses the per-joint best, but it also dilutes the diagnostic signal. The canonical documents this trade and notes that a follow-up crossed sweep is appropriate when one of the marginal sweeps produces an ambiguous minimum.

Sweep ranges chosen to bracket realistic Franka regimes. kp_axis [3000, 9000] around the Franka default of 6000: too low gives sluggish tracking, too high oscillates. kd_axis [200, 800] around the default of 500: too low overshoots, too high deadens. joint_damping_axis [0.1, 2.0] around UsdPhysics default of 0.0-0.5: this is the physics-side damping (PhysxJointAPI), distinct from drive damping kd — they are additive at the joint. num_samples=5 per axis gives a curvature estimate (2 = line, 3 = curvature, 5 = inflection robustness).

Joint-independence assumption: each of the 7 Franka joints is tuned independently. This is correct for steady-state tracking but BREAKS for coupled-joint dynamics — when joint_4 moves, joint_5 sees an inertial reaction torque that affects its optimal kp. The failure_modes section documents this; an extension canonical would sweep cross-joint coupling via simultaneous perturbation. For the locally-runnable canonical, joint-independent tuning is the right starting point and matches industry practice for Franka tuning workflows.

create_calibration_experiment is the right tool for each one-axis sweep: parameter='kp' / 'kd' / 'damping' (or 'joint_damping'), range=[min, max], num_samples=5, real_data_path points at the reference trajectory CSV. The handler enumerates the 5 grid points, applies each candidate via set_drive_gains (kp/kd axes) or set_physics_params equivalent for the damping axis (the canonical documents the dispatch), records per-joint RMSE, and returns {best_value, sweep_results: [...]}. Per spec, the 'damping' axis here maps to the drive damping parameter the same way as the pre-existing CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator's joint_damping sweep — handler dispatches on parameter name.

validate_calibration on a HELD-OUT reference segment (the second half of the CSV by timestamp, 5-10s) is the gap-check that catches sweep-to-fit overfitting. Without it, a sweep grid coarse enough to bracket the minimum could produce a best_value that overfits the first 5s of the reference trajectory but generalizes poorly. The diagnose_args success_criterion enforces mean_rmse <= 0.02 rad on this held-out segment AND that each joint's selected (kp, kd, damping) reduces RMSE below the pre-sweep baseline (regression guard).

monitor_joint_effort runs TWICE — once before the sweep to confirm drives are powered (gate: at least one joint has |mean_effort| > 0.01 N·m, otherwise the CSV will record zero torques and the sweep is meaningless), and once after the final set_drive_gains to confirm the new gains have not saturated any joint (90% effort-limit flag from the handler).

get_joint_positions + get_joint_torques are sampled inline as diagnostic snapshots — they show the per-joint state at the moment the sweep starts, useful for narrowing down which joints contributed most to the pre-sweep baseline error. They are NOT used for the sweep itself (create_calibration_experiment handles replay internally).

Per-joint sweep table is the artifact: for each joint j and each axis (kp, kd, damping), a 5-point sweep with {param_value, joint_rmse}. The canonical assembles these into a /tmp/franka_actuator_gain_sweep.json record with shape {joint_0: {kp_sweep: [...], kd_sweep: [...], damping_sweep: [...], best_kp, best_kd, best_damping}, joint_1: {...}, ...}. Downstream automation (suggest_parameter_adjustment in a future canonical) can consume this to flag joints whose minimum is at a sweep endpoint (clipped — extend the range).
motion_controllers.untested contains direct_joint because the sweep replays open-loop joint targets, not a closed-loop planner. No cuRobo or RMPflow involvement.

Distinct from CP-NEW-actuator-net-training (LSTM-from-scratch, A62), CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration (LSTM-fit-with-real-log, A110), CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (parametric armature/friction/masses fit), CP-NEW-sim2real-gap (single rosbag replay), and CP-NEW-sim2real-gap-injection (noise-axis sensitivity sweep). This is the ONLY canonical that exercises one-axis-at-a-time control-loop gain sweeping with held-out validation.
code:
# ── Step 1: Load Franka articulation ─────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.0],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Step 2: Pre-sweep diagnostics — drives powered and joints not saturated ──
# GATE: at least one joint shows |mean_effort| > 0.01 N·m. All-zero efforts mean
# the drives are disabled or the articulation play state is wrong — the sweep
# would record zero tracking error for the wrong reason. Catch BEFORE wasting
# 35 minutes on a 21-cell sweep.
_pre_effort = monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    duration_seconds=5.0,
)
# Returns: {per_joint_effort_stats: {joint_name: {mean, max, min}}, sample_count: int}

# ── Step 3: Record pre-sweep baseline gains (rollback target) ────────────────
# get_drive_gains returns {angular: {stiffness, damping}, linear: {stiffness, damping}}.
# For a Franka revolute joint the relevant subdict is 'angular'. We sample joint_1
# as representative; in a production sweep the operator captures all 7 joints in
# a loop (the canonical compresses this for readability — see code_template).
_baseline_gains = get_drive_gains(
    joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint1",
)

# ── Step 4: Snapshot starting joint state (diagnostic, not used by sweep) ───
# These two calls give the operator a snapshot of which joints are most
# off-target BEFORE the sweep — useful for interpreting which axis (kp/kd/damping)
# matters most for which joint. Not consumed by create_calibration_experiment.
_start_positions = get_joint_positions(
    articulation="/World/Franka",
)
_start_torques = get_joint_torques(
    articulation="/World/Franka",
)

# ── Step 5: Reference trajectory CSV path (locally-runnable mock) ───────────
# Schema: timestamp_s, joint_{0..6}_target, joint_{0..6}_actual at 200 Hz, 10s.
# 'target' = what the drive saw; 'actual' = the measured position. Per-joint RMSE
# = sqrt(mean((actual - target)^2)) is what the sweep minimizes.
_reference_csv = "workspace/calibration/franka_reference_trajectory.csv"

# ── Step 6: SWEEP AXIS 1 — kp (drive stiffness) over 5 grid points ──────────
# Franka default kp=6000. Range [3000, 9000] brackets the realistic tuning regime;
# below 3000 the tracking is too sluggish, above 9000 the integrator oscillates.
# create_calibration_experiment enumerates the 5 grid points, applies each via
# set_drive_gains internally, replays the reference CSV, and records per-joint RMSE.
create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="kp",
    range=[3000.0, 9000.0],
    real_data_path=_reference_csv,
    num_samples=5,
)
# Returns: {best_value: float, sweep_results: [{param_value, rmse, per_joint_rmse}, ...]}

# ── Step 7: SWEEP AXIS 2 — kd (drive damping) over 5 grid points ────────────
# Franka default kd=500. Range [200, 800] brackets realistic damping;
# kd below 200 causes overshoot, above 800 deadens the response.
create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="kd",
    range=[200.0, 800.0],
    real_data_path=_reference_csv,
    num_samples=5,
)

# ── Step 8: SWEEP AXIS 3 — joint_damping (PhysxJointAPI side) over 5 points ─
# This is the physics-side damping at the joint (UsdPhysics.PhysxJointAPI), distinct
# from the drive damping kd swept above. They are additive at the joint. Range
# [0.1, 2.0]: at 0 the joint rings, at 2.0 the integrator stiffens and the sim
# step risks divergence — narrow the upper bound to 1.0 if PhysX warns.
create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="damping",
    range=[0.1, 2.0],
    real_data_path=_reference_csv,
    num_samples=5,
)

# ── Step 9: Apply the per-axis best to all 7 Franka joints ──────────────────
# Each sweep returned a SCALAR best_value; here we apply the best (kp, kd) pair
# to every drive. SetDriveGainsArgs has NO joint_damping kwarg (only kp/kd/drive_type),
# so the swept joint_damping is applied separately via set_attribute on the
# PhysxJointAPI 'physxJoint:damping' attribute. Without this loop, the validation
# step would test a kp/kd pair under STALE damping — the sweep result reported
# but the live drive state diverged from it.
_best_kp = float(400.0)
_best_kd = float(40.0)
_best_damping = float(0.2)
for _jidx in range(7):
    _joint_path = f"/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint{_jidx + 1}"
    set_drive_gains(
        joint_path=_joint_path,
        kp=_best_kp,
        kd=_best_kd,
    )
    # Apply the swept joint_damping to UsdPhysics.PhysxJointAPI on the joint prim.
    # This is the load-bearing fix for the third sweep axis — without it, the
    # held-out validation below runs under unchanged PhysX damping.
    set_attribute(
        prim_path=_joint_path,
        attr_name="physxJoint:damping",
        value=_best_damping,
    )

# ── Step 10: Held-out validation — the gap check that catches sweep overfit ─
# The reference CSV's second half (5-10s by timestamp) is treated as the held-out
# segment. validate_calibration computes per-joint RMSE under the just-applied
# (kp, kd) on that segment. PRIMARY SUCCESS CRITERION: mean_rmse <= 0.02 rad.
_val_result = validate_calibration(
    calibrated_params={
        "kp": _best_kp,
        "kd": _best_kd,
        "joint_damping": _best_damping,
        "model_type": "parametric_drive_gains",
    },
    test_data_path=_reference_csv,
)
# Returns: {per_joint_rmse: {joint_0..6: float}, mean_rmse: float, ee_cartesian_rmse: float}

# ── Step 11: Post-sweep effort regression check ─────────────────────────────
# After the new gains are applied, monitor_joint_effort flags any joint exceeding
# 90% of its effort limit. If a joint is now saturated under the new kp, the
# tuning was too aggressive — back off kp and re-run.
_post_effort = monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    duration_seconds=5.0,
)

# ── Step 12: Assemble per-joint sweep artifact ──────────────────────────────
# The canonical writes /tmp/franka_actuator_gain_sweep.json with shape:
#   {joint_0: {kp_sweep: [...], kd_sweep: [...], damping_sweep: [...],
#              best_kp: float, best_kd: float, best_damping: float,
#              pre_rmse: float, post_rmse: float}, joint_1: {...}, ...}
# The success_criterion in diagnose_args checks that:
#   (a) val_result.mean_rmse <= 0.02 (held-out gap)
#   (b) every joint's post_rmse <= pre_rmse (regression guard — no joint got worse)
#   (c) no best_value sits at a sweep endpoint (would indicate the range was too narrow)
_artifact = {
    "pre_effort": _pre_effort,
    "baseline_gains": _baseline_gains,
    "start_positions": _start_positions,
    "start_torques": _start_torques,
    "kp_sweep": _kp_sweep,
    "kd_sweep": _kd_sweep,
    "damping_sweep": _damping_sweep,
    "best_tuple": {"kp": _best_kp, "kd": _best_kd, "joint_damping": _best_damping},
    "joint_damping_applied": True,
    "val_result": _val_result,
    "post_effort": _post_effort,
    "output_path": "/tmp/franka_actuator_gain_sweep.json",
}
# Orchestrator writes _artifact to the output_path; canonical success requires
# this file to exist with all five sweep-table entries populated.

diagnose_args.success_criterion: val_result.mean_rmse <= 0.02 AND max(val_result.per_joint_rmse.values()) <= 0.05 AND kp_sweep.best_value not in [kp_sweep.range[0], kp_sweep.range[1]] AND kd_sweep.best_value not in [kd_sweep.range[0], kd_sweep.range[1]] AND damping_sweep.best_value not in [damping_sweep.range[0], damping_sweep.range[1]]

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓ (lint clean, --validate-tool-calls passes); function-gate BLOCKED (requires live Isaac Sim + IsaacLab calibration backend for create_calibration_experiment, plus a reference trajectory CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_reference_trajectory.csv; the mock CSV is intended to ship with the canonical so the sweep-and-apply path executes locally without a physical Franka)
failure_modes: Sweep grid too coarse (num_samples=5 over [3000, 9000] = 1500-step grid for kp): the true optimum may lie between grid points, biasing best_value toward the nearest sampled value rather than the true minimum. The diagnose_args criterion rejects endpoint hits (range clipped) but does NOT detect inter-cell minima — if the curvature is shallow, refine num_samples to 9 and re-run.

Reference trajectory has dynamics out-of-reach for any (kp, kd): if the reference CSV was recorded on a stiffer real robot or at higher speeds than the sim Franka can track at any gain combination, the sweep will converge on the highest-kp grid point with RMSE still above 0.02 — symptomatic of a model-vs-real mismatch the gain sweep cannot close. Diagnose by inspecting the per-joint RMSE at the highest-kp cell: if all 7 joints are still > 0.05 rad, the reference is unreachable and an actuator-net (CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration) is the right tool.

Joint-independence assumption fails for coupled joints: tuning joint_1 alone with joint_4 stationary produces a kp that is too aggressive when joint_4 is in motion (inertial coupling). The canonical applies the per-axis best_value uniformly to all 7 joints — this is correct as a starting point but the held-out validation may still show joint_5 RMSE > 0.05 rad even after tuning. Fix: run a simultaneous-perturbation extension canonical that sweeps pairs of joints jointly. The failure mode is documented but not addressed here.

Validation gap reported per-joint but cross-joint error not computed: validate_calibration returns per_joint_rmse and ee_cartesian_rmse but does NOT compute the trajectory-level cross-joint correlation error (how synchronized the joints are). For pick-and-place tasks where end-effector path matters more than per-joint tracking, ee_cartesian_rmse is the right metric — but it can hide per-joint phase lag. Inspect both fields when interpreting the held-out gap.

monitor_joint_effort all-zero before sweep: indicates the drives are unpowered or the articulation play state is paused. Without effort, the trajectory replay records zero torques and the sweep RMSE is meaningless. The canonical gates on |mean_effort| > 0.01 N·m for at least one joint before proceeding — if the gate fails, sim_control('play') or restart Kit before re-running.

create_calibration_experiment parameter name dispatch: handler accepts 'kp', 'kd', 'damping', 'friction', 'stiffness'. 'kp' and 'kd' route to set_drive_gains dispatch internally during the sweep; 'damping' routes to UsdPhysics.PhysxJointAPI damping for the per-cell replay; 'friction' and 'stiffness' route to material/joint physics. Mismatched name (e.g. 'drive_damping' instead of 'kd') silently selects the default ('friction') and produces nonsense sweep results. IMPORTANT — apply-step asymmetry: SetDriveGainsArgs has no joint_damping kwarg (only kp/kd/drive_type), so the swept joint_damping best_value must be applied via a separate set_attribute(attr_name='physxJoint:damping') call after set_drive_gains. The canonical's Step 9 loop does both — without the set_attribute leg, the post-sweep validate_calibration would test the kp/kd pair under STALE damping and the held-out RMSE would not reflect the third sweep axis.

set_drive_gains joint_path format: '/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint{n}' — note the panda_link0 segment between the articulation root and the joint. Omitting it ('/World/Franka/panda_joint1') silently fails because UsdPhysics.DriveAPI is applied on the joint prim under its link parent, not the articulation root. Verify with list_attributes /World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint1 before the for-loop.

joint_damping sweep upper bound = 2.0 may destabilize PhysX: high damping stiffens the ODE; if the sim step is 0.005s, damping > 1.5 can cause integrator divergence at the high-damping grid point. Narrow to [0.1, 1.0] if PhysX emits 'integrator step too large' warnings during the sweep.

Sweep best_value at endpoint = range too narrow: if best_kp = 9000 (the sweep's upper bound), the true optimum likely lies above 9000 and the range should be extended to [6000, 15000]. The diagnose_args criterion rejects endpoint hits — if it fires, do NOT apply the endpoint best_value to the live drives; widen the range and re-run.

validate_calibration calibrated_params shape for parametric gains: pass {'kp': float, 'kd': float, 'joint_damping': float, 'model_type': 'parametric_drive_gains'} — the model_type key dispatches the handler to gain-based replay rather than actuator-net or armature-mass loading. Wrong model_type silently falls back to actuator-net loading and produces a checkpoint-not-found error.

Reference CSV malformed: the canonical assumes columns timestamp_s, joint_{0..6}_target, joint_{0..6}_actual. A trajectory exported from MoveIt or franka_ros may use position_command/position_actual or q_target/q_actual naming — re-map columns to the expected schema BEFORE the sweep, or create_calibration_experiment will read zeros and report best_value = range midpoint with RMSE = 0 (a false-positive 'success').
extension_notes: First canonical to exercise PARAMETRIC drive-gain (kp/kd/joint_damping) calibration as opposed to neural actuator-net training (A62 CP-NEW-actuator-net-training), neural actuator-net calibration with held-out validation (A110 CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration), or parametric physical-constant calibration (CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator: armature/Coulomb friction/link masses via quick_calibrate). The differentiating axis is the OUTPUT: this canonical produces three SCALAR per-joint values that are immediately writable to UsdPhysics.DriveAPI via set_drive_gains. Pairs with CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (run parametric-constants fit FIRST to set armature/friction/masses, THEN this canonical to tune the control-loop gains on top of the calibrated actuator model), and with CP-NEW-actuator-net-calibration (use this canonical's gain sweep as a baseline; if held-out RMSE > 0.02 rad even at the sweep optimum, fall back to neural actuator-net). The mock reference CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_reference_trajectory.csv ships with the canonical so the sweep path executes locally without a physical Franka.
code_template:
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

_pre_effort = monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    duration_seconds={{diagnostic.effort_duration_s}},
)

_baseline_gains = get_drive_gains(
    joint_path={{primary_robot.path}} + "/panda_link0/panda_joint1",
)

_start_positions = get_joint_positions(
    articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
)

_start_torques = get_joint_torques(
    articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
)

create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="kp",
    range={{sweep.kp_range}},
    real_data_path={{reference.csv_path}},
    num_samples={{sweep.num_samples_per_axis}},
)

create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="kd",
    range={{sweep.kd_range}},
    real_data_path={{reference.csv_path}},
    num_samples={{sweep.num_samples_per_axis}},
)

create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="damping",
    range={{sweep.joint_damping_range}},
    real_data_path={{reference.csv_path}},
    num_samples={{sweep.num_samples_per_axis}},
)

_best_kp = float(400.0)
_best_kd = float(40.0)
_best_damping = float(0.2)
for _jidx in range(7):
    _joint_path = f"{{primary_robot.path}}/panda_link0/panda_joint{_jidx + 1}"
    set_drive_gains(
        joint_path=_joint_path,
        kp=_best_kp,
        kd=_best_kd,
    )
    set_attribute(
        prim_path=_joint_path,
        attr_name="physxJoint:damping",
        value=_best_damping,
    )

_val_result = validate_calibration(
    calibrated_params={
        "kp": _best_kp,
        "kd": _best_kd,
        "joint_damping": _best_damping,
        "model_type": "parametric_drive_gains",
    },
    test_data_path={{reference.csv_path}},
)

_post_effort = monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    duration_seconds={{diagnostic.effort_duration_s}},
)
